questions.question: What are public facilities?
questions.correct_answer: Public facilities are services provided by the government for the benefit of all citizens.
questions.explanation: These services help people meet basic needs and improve quality of life.

questions.question: Give two examples of public facilities.
questions.explanation: These facilities are provided so that everyone can access essential services.

questions.question: Why is clean drinking water considered an important public facility?
questions.correct_answer: Because it is essential for health and survival.
questions.explanation: Safe water prevents diseases and improves living conditions.

questions.question: Who is responsible for providing public facilities?
questions.explanation: The government ensures that essential services are available to all citizens.

questions.question: Why can't private companies alone provide all public facilities?
questions.correct_answer: Because private companies mainly focus on profit.
questions.explanation: Essential services must be accessible to everyone, not just those who can pay.

questions.question: What problem occurs when public facilities are not equally distributed?
questions.explanation: Poor communities may suffer due to lack of basic facilities.

questions.question: How do taxes help provide public facilities?
questions.correct_answer: Taxes collected from citizens are used by the government to fund public services.
questions.explanation: These funds help maintain infrastructure and services.

questions.question: Give one example of inequality in public facilities.
questions.correct_answer: Some slum areas may not have proper water supply or sanitation.
questions.explanation: Unequal access affects health and quality of life.

questions.question: How can citizens help maintain public facilities?
questions.correct_answer: By using resources responsibly and supporting community efforts.
questions.explanation: Responsible usage ensures facilities remain available for everyone.

questions.question: Why are public facilities important for development?
questions.correct_answer: Because they improve health, education and living standards.
questions.explanation: Good public services support economic and social progress.

notes.title: Public Facilities
notes.content: Public facilities are essential services provided by the government to improve people's quality of life.

notes.title: Examples of Public Facilities
notes.content: Water supply, sanitation, electricity, transport, schools and hospitals are common public facilities.

notes.title: Government Responsibility
notes.content: The government ensures that essential services are available and accessible to all citizens.

notes.title: Inequality in Access
notes.content: Some communities may not receive equal access to facilities like water and sanitation.

notes.title: Funding Public Facilities
notes.content: Public facilities are usually funded through taxes collected by the government.

facts.fact: Clean drinking water is one of the most important public facilities for health.
Public facilities are funded mainly through taxes paid by citizens.
Unequal access to facilities can create social and economic inequalities.
Public facilities are essential for national development and welfare.
